summary: Gruber announces that he has been working full-time at Joyent, a startup building web-based applications for small businesses, since the beginning of 2005. He clarifies that Daring Fireball is not his full-time occupation, joking that readers might expect more regular posting if it were. He mentions Joyent's presence at the Web 2.0 Conference and credits collaborators Cameron Moll and Paige Pooler for their work on the Joyent website.
key_points: Gruber confirms Daring Fireball is a side project, not his full-time job
He joined Joyent full-time in early 2005, shifting away from freelance design and development
Joyent is building web-based applications for small businesses in the Web 2.0 era
The Joyent website was a collaborative effort with designer Cameron Moll and illustrator Paige Pooler
money_quotes: No, I do not write Daring Fireball full-time. (One would hope the publication schedule would be slightly less erratic if I were.)
I'm no longer working as a freelance designer and developer.
We're so "Web 2.0" that we're at the Web 2.0 Conference.
key_insight: In 2005, Gruber transitioned from freelancing to full-time startup work at Joyent while maintaining Daring Fireball as a side project, offering a glimpse into the early Web 2.0 startup era.
